FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Feb-18-2015 Continued To Mar-18-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Attorney to appear on March 18, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why the accounting petition has not been filed. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Post-hearing it was discovered that the accounting petition had been e-filed on 2/17/15 and entered into IJS on 2/18/15. The status hearing continued to March 18, 2015 is being vacated.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Zambrana. (Not Reported)

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Dec-18-2014 Continued To Feb-18-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT
Off Calendar Nov-12-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Helen Baumann for and with Armando Gonzales. Adora Gonzales was also present.

Ms. Baumann stated that the Trust only contains real property, that previously it was thought the property might be needed for Narcisa Gonzales, that Narcissa Gonzales is deceased so that is no longer the case, that Mr. Gonzales has been using his personal resources to pay taxes (etc), that preparing an accounting would cost quite a bit of money, and that her client wishes to waive the accounting and end the Court supervision. At the conclusion of Ms. Baumann's comments, the Court took the matter under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy McConico. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT
Off Calendar Sep-22-2014 Continued To Nov-03-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT
Off Calendar Sep-08-2014 Continued To Sep-22-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-24-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Helen Bauman for Armando Gonzales.

Ms. Bauman stated that the Kern County and the Fresno properties (lots) are not marketable right now, that they have negotiated a three year agreement with Lansing (real estate developing company) on the Antelope property which will generate some cash in the next couple of years, and that it is her understanding that the Antelope property is the only property that is not in the Trust. In addition, Ms. Bauman stated that Armando is paying all of the expenses, that there are minimal assets in Narcisa's conservatorship, that Narcisa's income is enough for her care needs and that Armando is satisfied to keep paying for the expenses. The Court stated that it is up to Counsel and the Parties to request that the Trust not remain under Court supervision if it is appropriate. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Mar-19-2013 Off Calendar

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Feb-06-2013 Continued To Mar-19-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Helen Baumann for and with Armando Gonzales (Trustee). Other Family members were also present.

Ms. Baumann stated that she had discussion with the Family members that are present and that they are committed to providing information to her so that she can get the accounting done. Status on the accounting was continued to March 19, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Dec-20-2012 Continued To Feb-06-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-06-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PCN-08-291881 Conservatorship of Narcisa E. Gonzales.

Appearances: Rowena Navia for Conservatee. Shiela Robello for and with Leo Bautista. Helen Bauman for Armando Gonzales.

Ms. Robello addressed the issue of fees, reported as to the Conservatee's care and as to the Case Manager's services. The accounting petitions and the fee petition were taken under submission. Status of case management was set for June 30, 2011 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 204. A declaration from Mr. Bautista regarding time spent and the issues dealt with is to be filed by June 23, 2011.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Order Appointing Leo F. Bautista As Successor Trustee, Bringing Trust Under Continuing Court Supervision; For Waiver Of Trustee'S Bond
Off Calendar Jan-11-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Helen Brennan Bauman for Armando Gonzales and Adora Gonzalez (Conservators of the Person). Rowena Navia for Narcisa Gonzales (Conservatee). Sheila Robello for Leo Bautista (Petitioner).

Ms. Bauman waived notice on behalf of Armando Gonzales and stated that the Family members refused to step down. The Parties expressed concern with regard to how the assets were being handled, lack of cooperation, the condition of the house, the repairs needed in the kitchen, the property not accruing income, the mortgage payments, unanswered questions with expenditures, the income tax returns, gambling debts, what was in the best interest of the Conservatee and future care needs/expenses. The Court ordered that the trust is to be brought under Court supervision, all powers of attorney currently in place are suspended. Until the issue of breach of Fiduciary duties is established, there is no cause to appoint Leo Bautista as Successor Trustee. The First Account on the Trust beginning June 1, 2009 is to be filed by February 5, 2011. Ms. Robello is to prepare the order and set the status date for filing of the Inventory and Appraisal. The Court will leave it up to Ms. Robello and Ms. Navia to remain in communication with Ms. Bauman as to what is going on with the Hayward property.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
